mission: Use the primary isolated-horizon U(1) construction to determine whether puncture labels can alter the horizon bundle degree, or only connection data on a fixed bundle.

theorem_1_fixed_u1_bundle.statement: In the standard isolated-horizon quantization, the horizon U(1) bundle Q is fixed by the intrinsic horizon geometry; the puncture data enter as connection/holonomy data on the punctured sphere S-V, not as changes of bundle isomorphism class. Therefore punctures do not by themselves vary c1(Q).
theorem_1_fixed_u1_bundle.exact_object: fixed horizon U(1) bundle Q with punctured-sphere connection data W on S-V

theorem_2_puncture_labels_cannot_change_.statement: Since first Chern class is a bundle isomorphism invariant and the standard puncture labels vary only the connection state on the fixed horizon bundle, no theorem using only the standard puncture labels a_i can generate a new bundle degree. This kills both additive and non-additive puncture-label-only routes to n_punct.

imports.additive_kill: additive lift from neutral Z_k puncture charges to integer degree

theorem_3_exact_remaining_positive_route.status: derived / strongest exact current frontier reduction
theorem_3_exact_remaining_positive_route.statement: After this fixed-bundle reduction, the exact remaining positive route is no longer a puncture-label lift. It is a theorem deriving a charged fermionic spin^c lift of the IO bridge into the fixed horizon U(1) bundle sector, or an equivalent new puncture-resolved operator sector carrying that charge.
theorem_3_exact_remaining_positive_route.exact_missing_object: fermionic charged lift into fixed horizon U(1) bundle Q

final_boundary.status: derived / strongest current Paper 35 charged-lift boundary
final_boundary.safe_statement: The puncture-label route is exhausted. Standard puncture data alter connection states on a fixed horizon U(1) bundle and cannot by themselves produce the nonzero index degree needed for Q5. The only remaining positive route is a new charged fermionic lift into that fixed U(1) sector, which is not present on the current stack.
